q: Which sequence correctly describes synaptic transmission at a cholinergic synapse?
options: Postsynaptic neuron depolarizes → vesicles fuse → ACh released → receptor binding
Action potential arrives at axon terminal → Ca2+ channels open → vesicles fuse with membrane → ACh released into cleft → binds postsynaptic receptors → EPSP generated
ACh diffuses across synapse → presynaptic membrane depolarizes → vesicles fill with neurotransmitter
Reuptake pumps release ACh → postsynaptic receptors open → K+ enters postsynaptic cell
explanation: Arrival of an action potential opens voltage-gated Ca2+ channels; Ca2+ influx triggers vesicle fusion and ACh release into the synaptic cleft where it binds receptors to generate an EPSP.
